security_concerns.description: Plaintext password stored in profiles.password column and compared directly (storedPassword != password). Fallback auth path at line 137 bypasses Supabase Auth entirely — no hashing used. (known/acceptable for dev)

Fallback authentication bypasses Supabase Auth session creation. auth.uid() will be NULL in RLS policies during fallback mode, causing some write operations (audit_logs, corrections) to potentially fail. (RLS off — known/acceptable)

No input validation on userId parameter when constructing file path '$userId/avatar.jpg'. If a caller passes a non-UUID value (e.g. '../../../etc'), it could cause path traversal in the Supabase Storage bucket. Should validate UUID v4 format.
No crash/error reporting setup. Missing FlutterError.onError, PlatformDispatcher.onError, or runZonedGuarded. Production release builds will silently drop unhandled exceptions.

logic_errors.description: Finance role routes (financeMerchantDetail, financeParentDetail) reuse AdminMerchantDetailScreen and AdminParentDetailScreen. These admin-named screens may expose admin-level UI controls (delete, elevated privileges) to petugas_keuangan users — verify these screens have no admin-specific actions.

suggestions.description: Force-unwrap (!) on pathParameters['studentId'] etc. at lines 290, 298, 444, 451, 459, 466, 513, 520, 527, 535. Safe today because GoRouter guarantees the param, but a null-aware access or fallback would be more defensive against future refactoring.

If AdminMerchantDetailScreen and AdminParentDetailScreen are truly role-agnostic, consider renaming them to avoid confusion. If they contain admin-specific controls, create separate screens for the finance role.
No timeout set on NFC startSession. Consider adding a 30-second application-level timeout with automatic session stop to prevent resource leaks if user never taps a card.
Add UUID v4 regex validation on userId before constructing file path to prevent potential path traversal in the Supabase Storage bucket.
Integrate error reporting (Sentry / Firebase Crashlytics) and configure FlutterError.onError + PlatformDispatcher.onError for production crash visibility.

summary: Core routing and service architecture is clean and well-structured with proper role-based guards, but the auth service's plaintext-password fallback and StorageService's unvalidated userId path represent real (if currently tolerated) security concerns, and production error reporting is entirely absent.
